question_html: What do you know about WWW? Explain in some sentences.
solution_html:
The World Wide Web (WWW) is a system of interlinked hypertext documents and multimedia content accessed via the internet. It was developed by Tim Berners-Lee in 1989 and launched in 1991. Users can access websites and web pages through web browsers by entering URLs. The WWW uses protocols like HTTP (Hypertext Transfer Protocol) and HTTPS (HTTP Secure) to transmit data. It revolutionized information sharing and communication by allowing users to easily navigate and interact with a vast array of content, making it an integral part of modern life and digital interaction.

Marks Distribution:

For describing the WWW (World Wide Web) system; 01 mark.

For mentioning the developer's name; 0.5 mark.

For mentioning development time and launch time; 0.5 mark.

For writing about the benefits of the WWW; 0.5 mark.

[N.B: 0.5 mark will be deducted for one or two (spelling or grammatical) mistakes, 01 mark for three or more (spelling or grammatical) mistakes.]

question_html:
Write down meaningful sentences with each of the following words:

Poverty, Innovation, Transparency, Bureaucracy, Leadership
solution_html:
Poverty: Eradicating poverty is a major goal of the SDGs.

Innovation: Innovation drives progress in technology and industry.

Transparency: Transparency in governance builds public trust.

Bureaucracy: A complex bureaucracy can delay development projects.

Leadership: Effective leadership is vital during national emergencies.

Marks Distribution:

For making five meaningful sentences; (0.5× 5) = 2.5 marks.
